commands.review_summary:
The branch adds six primary smoke-ci taxonomy coverage IDs to existing QA scenarios, adds the channel routing category to the release profile, and removes a brittle hard-coded release profile assertion.

PR surface: Tests -20, Other +7. Total -13 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is QA scorecard metadata, not a user bug. The high-confidence check is source inspection of PR-head YAML against the current scorecard primary-evidence collector.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; this protected maintainer PR has no actionable patch finding and should proceed through normal maintainer review plus check gating.

target.title: Add smoke-ci primary scorecard evidence
target.body:
## Summary
- add primary smoke-ci scorecard evidence links for six high-value already-covered behaviors
- cover Control UI bootstrap, cron RPC/announce delivery, protected Prometheus scrape, media generation persistence, and thread placement
- make the `release` profile explicitly include all `smoke-ci` categories, plus its release-only OpenAI/Codex category
- keep this as metadata-only scenario coverage wiring; no release gate is added

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/coverage-report.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-packs.test.ts`
- `node --import tsx scripts/qa-coverage-report.ts --json`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/qa-lab/src/coverage-report.test.ts qa/scenarios/ui/control-ui-qa-channel-image-roundtrip.yaml qa/scenarios/scheduling/cron-one-minute-ping.yaml qa/scenarios/runtime/docker-prometheus-smoke.yaml qa/scenarios/media/image-generation-roundtrip.yaml qa/scenarios/channels/thread-follow-up.yaml`
- `git diff --check`

## Coverage impact
- unknown taxonomy coverage IDs remain `0`
- `smoke-ci`: `89 / 214` primary fulfilled (`41.6%`), `71` missing-primary warnings
- `release`: `91 / 219` primary fulfilled (`41.6%`), `73` missing-primary warnings
- `release` has no smoke-ci-only category gaps; its only extra category is `openai-codex-provider-path.responses-and-tool-compatibility`

## Review notes
- kept `dangerous-tool-safeguards` unclaimed because the existing denial scenario does not attempt a dangerous tool
- kept `image-and-pdf-input-routing` unclaimed because the existing media scenario only proves image attachment routing, not PDF routing
target.author: romneyda
